summary:
Protamines are not ubiquitously expressed; they are specifically localized to sperm cells during maturation and have no detectable expression in somatic tissues. This limited, germ-cell-specific role distinguishes them from widely expressed proteins like histones, confirming they lack universal distribution throughout the human body.  

$\boxed{\text{No}}$

This limited, germ-cell-specific role distinguishes them from widely expressed proteins like histones, confirming they lack universal distribution throughout the human body.  

$\boxed{\text{No}}$. Answer choice: B
